---
title: "Vidit_LDA_Exercise_WashU"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-06"
---

## Data Cleaning

```{R}
# Libraries
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{R, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Reading the datasets
# Baseline Data
base = read_csv(here("files", "baseline_df.csv"))
# Visits Data
vis = read_csv(here("files", "visits_df.csv"))
# Exits and Death Data
outcome = read_csv(here("files", "outcomes_df.csv"))

# Summary check for all datasets
summary(base)
print("---")
summary(vis)
print("---")
summary(outcome)

# View all of them
view(base)
view(vis)
view(outcome)

# Check if there are more than one exit dates
check_exit = outcome |>
  group_by(id) |>
  summarise(n_exit = n_distinct(exit_date)) |>
  count(n_exit)
```

```{R, warning = FALSE}
# Data Cleaning, Manipulation and Merging
# Ignoring NAs or missingnes for now
# First cleaning base
base = base |>
  mutate(sex = as.factor(sex))
summary(base$sex)
# So, sex = 1, M, male, Male means males and otherwise female
base_clean = base |>
  mutate(male = factor(
  ifelse(sex %in% c(1, "M", "male", "Male"), 1, 0),
  levels = c(0, 1)
)) |>
  dplyr::select(-sex) |>
  mutate(entry_date = as.Date(entry_date, format = "%d-%b-%Y")) |>
  mutate(id = as.character(id))
summary(base_clean)

# Now cleaning vis
vis = vis |>
  mutate(visit_type = as.factor(visit_type))
summary(vis$visit_type)

vis_clean = vis |>
  mutate(
    visit_type = factor(tolower(as.character(visit_type))),
    id = as.character(id),
    visit_date = as.Date(visit_date, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    across(starts_with("sbp"), ~ ifelse(.x <= 40, NA, .x)),
    across(starts_with("dbp"), ~ ifelse(.x <= 30, NA, .x))
  )
summary(vis_clean)

# Extract entry visit dates from visits
entry_visits = vis_clean |>
  filter(visit_type == "entry") |>
  select(id, visit_date) |>
  rename(entry_visit_date = visit_date)

# Compare with baseline entry_date
check_entry = base_clean |>
  select(id, entry_date) |>
  left_join(entry_visits, by = "id") |>
  mutate(
    match = entry_date == entry_visit_date
  )

# Summary of matches
table(check_entry$match, useNA = "ifany")
# Note: We only want to see TRUE- if there are any FALSE then it means some don't match.

# Now cleaning outcome 
outcome = outcome |>
  mutate(exit_type = as.factor(exit_type))
summary(outcome$exit_type)

outcome_clean = outcome |>
  mutate(id = as.character(id),
    exit_date = as.Date(exit_date, format = "%d/%m/%Y"))

summary(outcome_clean)

# Joining the data!
full_data = vis_clean|>
  left_join(base_clean, by="id")|>
  left_join(outcome_clean, by="id")


full_data = full_data |>
  mutate(
    avg_sbp = rowMeans(cbind(sbp2, sbp3), na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_dbp = rowMeans(cbind(dbp2, dbp3), na.rm = TRUE)
  )

full_data = full_data |>
  mutate(
  a_med = as.integer(
      if_any(
        c(amlodipine, bisoprolol, captopril, carvedilol,
          enalapril, furosemide, hctz, lisinopril,
          losaratan, metoprolol),
        ~ coalesce(.x, 0) == 1
      ) |
      str_detect(coalesce(tolower(free_text_med), ""), "ramipril|olmesartan")
    )
)
# Medicines that were not considered for a_med :-
# aspirin: antiplatelet (not antihypertensive)
# atorvastatin: statin (lipid-lowering agent, not antihypertensive)
# simvastatin: statin (lipid-lowering agent, not antihypertensive)
# metformin: antidiabetic medication (not antihypertensive)
# glyburide: antidiabetic medication (not antihypertensive)
# warfarin (free text): anticoagulant (not antihypertensive)

summary(full_data)
full_data <- full_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    id, age, male, height, weight, entry_date,
    visit_date, visit_type,
    sbp1, sbp2, sbp3, avg_sbp,
    dbp1, dbp2, dbp3, avg_dbp,
    amlodipine:free_text_med,
    a_med,
    everything()
  )

write_csv(full_data, "files/full_data.csv")

summary(as.factor(full_data$free_text_med))
# Apart from warfarin rest are drugs blood pressure drugs
# Note: I read warfarin is a blood thinner so not counting it
```

```{R, warning = FALSE}
head(full_data, 20)
```

## Incident Hypertension

```{R, warning = FALSE}
# Helper function
safe_min <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) return(as.Date(NA))
  min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# Initial cleaning (All visits : for Part B)
table_data_B = full_data |>
  arrange(id, visit_date) |>
  mutate(
    meds = a_med,
    htn = as.integer(
    (!is.na(avg_sbp) & avg_sbp >= 130) |
    (!is.na(avg_dbp) & avg_dbp >= 80)
    ),
    htn_or_meds = as.integer(htn == 1 | meds == 1)
  ) |>
  group_by(id) |>
  mutate(
    visit_number = row_number(),
    htn_cum = cumsum(htn_or_meds)   
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Create clinic-only version (for Part A)
table_data_A = table_data_B |>
  filter(visit_type != "home")

# Keep only those who were disease-free at visit 1
baseline_df_ids_A = table_data_A |>
  filter(visit_number == 1, htn == 0, meds == 0) |>
  pull(id)

table_data_A = table_data_A |>
  filter(id %in% baseline_df_ids_A)

# Calculate dates and Person-Years 
event_dates_A = table_data_A |>
  arrange(id, visit_date) |>
  group_by(id) |>
  summarise(
    entry_date = min(visit_date),
    last_visit = max(visit_date),
    exit_date = unique(exit_date),
    
    last_date = pmin(last_visit, exit_date, na.rm = TRUE),

    first_event_date = safe_min(visit_date[visit_number > 1 & htn_or_meds == 1]),
    second_event_date = safe_min(visit_date[visit_number > 1 & htn_cum >= 2])  
  ) |>
  mutate(
    py_A = as.numeric(pmin(first_event_date, last_date, na.rm = TRUE) - entry_date) / 365.25,
    py_B = as.numeric(pmin(second_event_date, last_date, na.rm = TRUE) - entry_date) / 365.25,
    event_A = as.integer(!is.na(first_event_date)),
    event_B = as.integer(!is.na(second_event_date))
  )

# Final person-level dataset (Part A)
person_df_A = table_data_A |>
  group_by(id) |>
  summarise(
    male = first(male),
    age = first(age)
  ) |>
  inner_join(event_dates_A, by = "id") |>
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      age < 30 ~ "18-29 years",
      age < 40 ~ "30-39 years",
      age < 50 ~ "40-49 years",
      age < 60 ~ "50-59 years",
      TRUE ~ "60+ years"
    )
  )

# Keep only those who were disease-free at visit 1
baseline_df_ids_B = table_data_B |>
  filter(visit_number == 1, htn == 0, meds == 0) |>
  pull(id)

table_data_B = table_data_B |>
  filter(id %in% baseline_df_ids_B)

# Calculate dates and Person-Years 
event_dates_B = table_data_B |>
  arrange(id, visit_date) |>
  group_by(id) |>
  summarise(
    entry_date = min(visit_date),
    last_visit = max(visit_date),
    exit_date = unique(exit_date),
    
    last_date = pmin(last_visit, exit_date, na.rm = TRUE),

    first_event_date = safe_min(visit_date[visit_number > 1 & htn_or_meds == 1]),
    second_event_date = safe_min(visit_date[visit_number > 1 & htn_cum >= 2])  
  ) |>
  mutate(
    py_A = as.numeric(pmin(first_event_date, last_date, na.rm = TRUE) - entry_date) / 365.25,
    py_B = as.numeric(pmin(second_event_date, last_date, na.rm = TRUE) - entry_date) / 365.25,
    event_A = as.integer(!is.na(first_event_date)),
    event_B = as.integer(!is.na(second_event_date))
  )

# Final person-level dataset (Part B)
person_df_B = table_data_B |>
  group_by(id) |>
  summarise(
    male = first(male),
    age = first(age)
  ) |>
  inner_join(event_dates_B, by = "id") |>
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      age < 30 ~ "18-29 years",
      age < 40 ~ "30-39 years",
      age < 50 ~ "40-49 years",
      age < 60 ~ "50-59 years",
      TRUE ~ "60+ years"
    )
  )
```


```{R, warning = FALSE}
make_table_kable = function(df, event_var) {
  
  # Which PY column to use?
  py_col = ifelse(event_var == "event_A", "py_A", "py_B")
  
  table_rows = list()
  
  # Total row
  n_total = nrow(df)
  e_total = sum(df[[event_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
  py_total = sum(df[[py_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  inc_total = ifelse(py_total > 0, (e_total / py_total) * 1000, NA)
  
  table_rows[[1]] = data.frame(
    Category = "**Total**",
    `Disease Free (n)` = n_total,
    `Events (n)` = e_total,
    `Person-Years` = round(py_total, 2),
    `Incidence per 1000 PYs` = round(inc_total, 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Sex header row
  table_rows[[2]] = data.frame(
    Category = "**Sex**",
    `Disease Free (n)` = "",
    `Events (n)` = "",
    `Person-Years` = "",
    `Incidence per 1000 PYs` = "",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Sex sub-rows
  for (sex in c(1,0)) {
    sub <- df[df$male == sex, ]
    py_sub <- sum(sub[[py_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
    inc_sub <- ifelse(py_sub > 0, (sum(sub[[event_var]], na.rm = TRUE) / py_sub) * 1000, NA)
    table_rows[[length(table_rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
      Category = ifelse(sex == 1, "Male", "Female"),
      `Disease Free (n)` = nrow(sub),
      `Events (n)` = sum(sub[[event_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      `Person-Years` = round(py_sub, 2),
      `Incidence per 1000 PYs` = round(inc_sub, 2),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  
  # Age group header row
  table_rows[[length(table_rows)+1]] = data.frame(
    Category = "**Age group**",
    `Disease Free (n)` = "",
    `Events (n)` = "",
    `Person-Years` = "",
    `Incidence per 1000 PYs` = "",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Age group sub-rows
  groups <- c("18-29 years","30-39 years","40-49 years","50-59 years","60+ years")
  for (g in groups) {
    sub <- df[df$age_group == g, ]
    if (nrow(sub) > 0) {
      py_sub <- sum(sub[[py_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
      inc_sub <- ifelse(py_sub > 0, (sum(sub[[event_var]], na.rm = TRUE) / py_sub) * 1000, NA)
      table_rows[[length(table_rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
        Category = g,
        `Disease Free (n)` = nrow(sub),
        `Events (n)` = sum(sub[[event_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
        `Person-Years` = round(py_sub, 2),
        `Incidence per 1000 PYs` = round(inc_sub, 2),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
  
  # Bind all rows
  out_table = do.call(rbind, table_rows)
  
  # Column names
  colnames(out_table) = c("Category","Disease Free (n)","Events (n)","Person-Years","Incidence per 1000 PYs")
  
  out_table %>%
    kbl(caption = paste0("Incident Hypertension - ", event_var),
        escape = FALSE,  # bold markdown works
        align = "lrrrr") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "left", 
                  bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"))
}

# Part A table
make_table_kable(person_df_A, "event_A")

# Part B table
make_table_kable(person_df_B, "event_B")
```

Incidence rates of hypertension were estimated using person-years of follow-up. Hypertension was defined as SBP ≥130 mmHg, DBP ≥80 mmHg, or antihypertensive medication use.

Part A defined incident hypertension as the first occurrence, while Part B used a stricter second-occurrence definition. Part A excluded home visits, whereas Part B included all visit types.

Only participants free of hypertension at baseline were included. Rates were stratified by sex and age.

Results: Incidence was higher in Part A (208.5 per 1000 PY) compared to Part B (103.8 per 1000 PY), reflecting the stricter outcome definition in Part B and differences in observed follow-up time due to inclusion of additional visit types. Age 18-29 had slightly smaller incidence rates compared to other age groups. The other groups were pretty similar in incidence rates. There was just a difference of approximately 1 incidence per 1000 PYs amongst males and females.

## Regression

```{R, warning = FALSE}
# Baseline weight
weight_df = vis_clean |>
  filter(visit_type == "entry") |>
  dplyr::select(id, weight)
# Create BMI
bmi_data = base_clean |>
  left_join(weight_df, by = "id") |>
  mutate(height = height / 100) |> # Height to meters
  mutate(bmi = weight / (height^2)) |>
  dplyr::select(id, bmi)
```

```{R, warning = FALSE}
person_df_A = person_df_A |>
  left_join(bmi_data, by = 'id') |>
  mutate(age10 = age/10)
```

```{R, message= FALSE, warning = FALSE}
person_df_A = person_df_A |>
  mutate(
    event_A = as.integer(event_A),
    py_A = as.numeric(py_A), 
    bmi_cat = case_when(
      bmi < 18.5 ~ "Underweight",
      bmi < 25 ~ "Normal",
      bmi < 30 ~ "Overweight",
      TRUE ~ "Obese"
    ),
    bmi_cat = factor(bmi_cat, levels = c("Normal", "Overweight", "Obese", "Underweight"))
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(event_A),
    !is.na(py_A),
    py_A > 0
  )

# Univariable models
model_age = glm(event_A ~ age10,
                offset = log(py_A),
                family = poisson(),
                data = person_df_A)

model_sex = glm(event_A ~ male,
                offset = log(py_A),
                family = poisson(),
                data = person_df_A)

model_bmi = glm(event_A ~ bmi_cat,
                offset = log(py_A),
                family = poisson(),
                data = person_df_A)

# Multivariable model
model_multi = glm(event_A ~ age10 + male + bmi_cat,
                  offset = log(py_A),
                  family = poisson(),
                  data = person_df_A)

# IRRs + CI function
get_results = function(model) {
  irr = exp(coef(model))
  ci = exp(confint(model))
  
  out = data.frame(
    Variable = names(irr),
    IRR = irr,
    CI_low = ci[,1],
    CI_high = ci[,2]
  )
  
  return(out)
}

# Build tables
tab_age = get_results(model_age)
tab_sex = get_results(model_sex)
tab_bmi = get_results(model_bmi)
tab_multi = get_results(model_multi)

tab_age |>
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Univariable Model: Age") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

tab_sex |>
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Univariable Model: Sex") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

tab_bmi |>
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Univariable Model: BMI Categories") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

tab_multi |>
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Multivariable Model") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

overdispersion_check = function(model) {
  ratio = sum(residuals(model, type = "pearson")^2) / df.residual(model)
  return(ratio)
}
overdispersion_check(model_age)
overdispersion_check(model_sex)
overdispersion_check(model_bmi)
overdispersion_check(model_multi)
```

Used: Poisson regression models with a log person-time offset to estimate incident rate ratios for incident hypertension.
Univariable model results:
1) Age (per 10-year increase) was not significantly associated with incident hypertension (IRR ≈ 1.00). (Plus, the confidence interval contains the null value of 1.)

2) There was no evidence of a difference in incidence between males and females (IRR ≈ 0.99). (Plus, the confidence interval contains the null value of 1. Note: male = 0 i.e. being female is the reference group here.)

3) Compared to normal BMI, overweight, obesity, and underweight categories were not significantly associated with hypertension incidence. (Plus, the confidence interval contains the null value of 1.)

Multivariable model results: Adjustment for age, sex, and BMI did not materially change estimates, and no covariates showed statistically significant associations.

Mild overdispersion was observed (~1.3), but was not considered substantial in all models. Considered not substantial. Kept poisson based models as they are simpler and more interpretive.
